rag_rules: Validator diagnostics must be resolved by identifying and fixing root causes in visible identifiers or type mismatches without altering unrelated architecture.
AGREE contracts should only reference visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex scope.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are visible in the current scope and types match between assumptions and guarantees.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes and avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE logical operators include `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>` for expressing guarantees.
Helper declarations must match the type of the expression they represent: int, real, or bool.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the analyzed model.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
